refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple cycle phases (load, travel, dump) into a single requirement, which implies multiple obligations. To be atomic and solution-neutral, it is necessary to separate each phase into its own FR. This refinement focuses on the load cycle as one atomic functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load cycle process steps to minimize time
Implement real-time monitoring and adaptive control of cycle time
Design modular load cycle stages to enable parallel operations
Incorporate buffer or staging areas to decouple cycle stages and reduce bottlenecks
Use predictive scheduling to anticipate and adjust cycle timing
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize load cycle process steps to minimize time

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on optimizing the process steps independently, which supports decoupling from other system functions and allows targeted improvements without introducing dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that cycle time can be improved primarily through process optimization rather than hardware changes
Risk that external factors (e.g., site conditions, operator variability) may limit achievable cycle time improvements
Assumes accurate measurement and feedback mechanisms are available to verify cycle time performance
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time monitoring or adaptive control as a complementary DP to handle variability and ensure cycle time targets are met under changing conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic and optimization algorithms for operational processes including load cycle optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational data and sends performance metrics related to load cycle optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and schedules for load cycle operations.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as optimization logic fits well within system solution; minimal diffusion as most related data and control remain internal.
Vehicle module is more about physical control and hardware; optimization logic would diffuse into other modules for data and commands.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; optimization logic would be external and require significant data exchange.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring; optimization logic unrelated and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the load cycle optimization DP, as it centralizes the process logic and minimizes diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some coupling.
If optimization requires frequent safety checks, some interface with Safety Services may be needed, increasing coupling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed; DP is well contained within NGHE Solution module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Optimize load cycle process steps to minimize time' directly addresses the FR of supporting the customer-defined cycle time target. The DP logically solves the requirement by focusing on process optimization to meet the cycle time acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target).

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion, effectively containing the DP. Other modules have lower containment scores and higher diffusion, indicating that the DP is well localized within the NGHE Solution module, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data measuring cycle time performance against the target under defined duty cycles and site conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership of the DP within the NGHE Solution module is clear, with well-defined interfaces for telemetry and tasking, supporting clear allocation of responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: No revision needed; DP is well contained within NGHE Solution module.

decision.justification: All gates have passed successfully, indicating that the current DP effectively addresses the FR, the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module minimizing diffusion risks, and the implementation module ownership and verification feasibility are clear. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current level without further decomposition to avoid unnecessary complexity and maintain clarity in verification and ownership.

assumptions: Telemetry data accurately reflects cycle time performance under defined duty cycles and site conditions.
The NGHE Solution module's high cohesion ensures minimal diffusion and manageable coupling despite dependencies on real-time data from other modules.
open_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some coupling, which needs monitoring.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data confirming cycle time performance meets target under operational conditions.
Verification results demonstrating the DP's effectiveness in process optimization.
